source_jobs.description:
<div class="content-intro"><p>Here at Appian, our values of <strong>Intensity </strong>and <strong>Excellence </strong>define who we are. We set high standards and live up to them, ensuring that everything we do is done with care and quality. We approach every challenge with ambition and commitment, holding ourselves and each other accountable to achieve the best results. When you join Appian, you’ll be part of a passionate team dedicated to accomplishing hard things, together.</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p></div><h2><span>&nbsp;</span>AI Security Engineer (Agentic SOC)</h2><h3></h3><h3>In this role, you will:</h3><ul><li><p>Build &amp; Deploy Agents:<span> Design, test, and deploy autonomous and semi-autonomous AI agents that integrate natively with our enterprise security stack (SIEM, EDR, XDR, and Threat Intel feeds).</span></p></li><li><p>Code the Playbooks:<span> Translate traditional, human-centric SOC playbooks and analyst workflows into deterministic and heuristic agentic pipelines (using DAGs and multi-agent routing).</span></p></li><li><p>Optimize RAG Pipelines:<span> Design, optimize, and maintain production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows to inject real-time security context, network topology, and historical incident logs into agent prompts.</span></p></li><li><p>LLM Performance Engineering:<span> Continuously evaluate, benchmark, and optimize LLM performance, context window utilization, latency, and cost-efficiency across various models (OSS and commercial).</span></p></li><li><p>Design Human-in-the-Loop (HITL):<span> Collaborate deeply with Tier 3 Analysts and Threat Hunters to engineer seamless HITL handoff mechanisms, ensuring agents safely escalate complex anomalies to humans.</span></p></li><li><p>Secure the AI:<span> Implement robust security boundaries around our LLM architecture, mitigating risks like prompt injection, data poisoning, model tool-abuse, and addressing the </span>OWASP Top 10 for LLMs<span>.</span></p></li></ul><p></p><h3>The Ideal Candidate:</h3><ul><li><p>You are a Builder First:<span> You take immense pride in shipping clean, production-grade, asynchronous code. You care about system architecture as much as model accuracy.</span></p></li><li><p>Security-Curious or Security-Hardened:<span> You bridge the gap between AI research and practical cybersecurity. You understand that an AI agent is only as good as the guardrails keeping it from deleting a production server during a false positive.</span></p></li><li><p>Thrives in Ambiguity:<span> Building an Agentic SOC means charting unknown territory. You love breaking down abstract, high-level security problems into concrete, execution-ready AI systems.</span></p></li></ul><h3>Basic Qualifications:</h3><ul><li><p><span>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or related fields plus at least 2 years of experience developing AI and ML algorithms or technologies, </span>OR<span> a Master's degree in a related field plus at least 2 years of experience.</span></p></li><li><p><span>At least 2 years of experience programming with Python, Go, Scala, or Java.</span></p></li></ul><h3>Preferred Qualifications:</h3><ul><li><p><span>3 years of experience deploying scalable and responsible AI solutions on cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud, Azu
